_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in B1/004 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Ben; computer plays Emma. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 004 · Helping the Environment

npc.text: Our town has a pollution problem.

routes.meaning: Ben agrees and mentions the dirty river.
examples: I know. The river is very dirty now.
I know. The river is really dirty now.
Yes, the river is very dirty now.
I know. The river is so dirty.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I know. The river is very dirty now.”

routes.meaning: Ben asks what is causing the problem.
examples: What is causing it?
Why is our town so polluted?
What kind of pollution?
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask “What is causing it?” or agree: “I know. The river is very dirty now.”

partial.message: Agree with Emma: “I know. The river is very dirty now.”

off_topic.message: Emma is worried about pollution. Say “I know. The river is very dirty now.”

npc.text: Litter and waste have made the river dirty.

routes.meaning: Ben agrees the river is dirty.
examples: I know. The river is very dirty now.
Yes, the river is very dirty now.
You're right, the river is very dirty.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I know. The river is very dirty now.”

partial.message: Say “I know. The river is very dirty now.”

off_topic.message: Tell Emma “The river is very dirty now.”

npc.text: We should do something to help.

routes.meaning: Ben suggests picking up trash every weekend.
examples: We could pick up trash every weekend.
We could pick up trash on weekends.
Let's pick up trash every weekend.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “We could pick up trash every weekend.”

routes.meaning: Ben asks how they could help.
examples: What could we do to help?
How can we help?
What should we do first?
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask “What could we do?” or suggest “We could pick up trash every weekend.”

partial.message: Suggest an idea: “We could pick up trash every weekend.”

off_topic.message: Emma wants to help. Say “We could pick up trash every weekend.”

npc.text: There are many ways. What could we do?

examples: We could pick up trash every weekend.
We could pick up trash on weekends.
Maybe we could pick up trash every weekend.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “We could pick up trash every weekend.”

partial.message: Suggest “We could pick up trash every weekend.”

off_topic.message: Tell Emma “We could pick up trash every weekend.”

npc.text: That's a good idea. We could also plant trees.

routes.meaning: Ben says they need more people to join.
examples: However, we need more people to join us.
But we need more people to join us.
However, we need more people to help.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “However, we need more people to join us.”

routes.meaning: Ben likes the tree idea and asks where.
examples: Great idea! Where could we plant them?
I love that. Where should we plant the trees?
Nice! Where can we plant trees?
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask about the trees, or say “However, we need more people to join us.”

partial.message: Raise a concern: “However, we need more people to join us.”

off_topic.message: Say what you need: “However, we need more people to join us.”

npc.text: We could plant trees along the river bank.

routes.meaning: Ben says they need more people to join.
examples: However, we need more people to join us.
But we need more people to join us.
We still need more people to join us.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “However, we need more people to join us.”

partial.message: Say “However, we need more people to join us.”

off_topic.message: Tell Emma “We need more people to join us.”

npc.text: Let's tell our friends and our class.

routes.meaning: Ben says if everyone helps, they can make a difference.
examples: If everyone helps, we can make a difference.
If everyone helps, we will make a difference.
If we all help, we can make a difference.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “If everyone helps, we can make a difference.”

routes.meaning: Ben asks how they can tell more people.
examples: How should we tell them?
How can we spread the word?
Should we make posters?
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask “How should we tell them?” or say “If everyone helps, we can make a difference.”

partial.message: Encourage everyone: “If everyone helps, we can make a difference.”

off_topic.message: Tell Emma “If everyone helps, we can make a difference.”

npc.text: We can make posters and share it online.

routes.meaning: Ben says if everyone helps, they can make a difference.
examples: If everyone helps, we can make a difference.
If everyone helps, we will make a difference.
Then if everyone helps, we can make a difference.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “If everyone helps, we can make a difference.”

partial.message: Say “If everyone helps, we can make a difference.”

off_topic.message: Tell Emma “If everyone helps, we can make a difference.”

npc.text: A clean town is better for everyone.

learning_objective: Agree and set a start time

examples: Let's start this Saturday!
Let's start this Saturday.
Great, let's start this Saturday!

routes.meaning: Ben wants to start right away.
examples: Let's start right now!
Why wait? Let's start today!
Let's begin today!
routes.hint: 💬 You can say “Let's start this Saturday!” or “Let's start right now!”

partial.message: Set a day: “Let's start this Saturday!”

off_topic.message: Emma agrees. Say “Let's start this Saturday!”

npc.text: Perfect! I'll bring bags and gloves. See you then!
Even better! Let's begin right now. Thank you, Ben!
